response:
1. THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES COMBINED IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 1st CIRCLE IS r, AND EACH SUCCESSIVE CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THAT THE RADIUS OF THE n-th CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^{n-1}. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² FOR n FROM 1 TO 8. THIS CAN BE WRITTEN AS π × r² × (1.1^0 + 1.1^1 + 1.1^2 + 1.1^3 + 1.1^4 + 1.1^5 + 1.1^6 + 1.1^7). THE FINAL EXPRESSION IS π × r² × (1.1^0 + 1.1^1 + 1.1^2 + 1.1^3 + 1.1^4 + 1.1^5 + 1.1^6 + 1.1^7).

2. THE TEXT IS PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^4. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π × r × (1.1)^4. THE TEXT IS SPACED 0.5 CM APART, AND THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS (INCLUDING SPACES) MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THIS IS 2π × r × (1.1)^4 / 0.5. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 4π × r × (1.1)^4. THE LETTER I APPEARS AT LEAST 11 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
